info.title: Twitter API - Tweet Replies V2
info.description: Get tweet replies with advanced sorting options.

type.description: Object kind of the record, distinguishing a tweet from other entities the API may return.

id.description: The reply tweet's unique identifier; use it to fetch this reply's own replies or to build its URL.

url.description: Direct, clickable link to the reply tweet.

text.description: Full body text of the reply.

source.description: The client application from which the reply was posted, such as a web or mobile app.

retweetCount.description: Number of times the reply has been retweeted.

replyCount.description: Number of replies this reply itself has received.

likeCount.description: Number of likes the reply has received.

quoteCount.description: Number of times the reply has been quote-tweeted.

viewCount.description: Number of times the reply has been viewed.

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the reply was posted.

lang.description: Detected language of the reply text, as a language code.

bookmarkCount.description: Number of times the reply has been bookmarked.

isReply.description: Whether this tweet is itself a reply to another tweet.

inReplyToId.description: Identifier of the tweet this reply is responding to.

conversationId.description: Identifier of the conversation thread the reply belongs to; typically the ID of the original tweet that started the thread.

inReplyToUserId.description: Identifier of the user being replied to.

inReplyToUsername.description: Handle of the user being replied to.

isLimitedReply.description: Whether replying to this tweet is restricted by the author's reply settings.

get.description: Fetch the replies to a given tweet, returned one page at a time and ordered by relevance, recency, or like count. Read-only; use the returned cursor to walk through subsequent pages.

parameters.name: tweetId
parameters.description: Identifier of the tweet whose replies you want to retrieve. It is the numeric ID found in the tweet's URL.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Pagination token pointing at the next batch of replies. Take it from the `next_cursor` of a previous response; leave it unset to start from the first page.

parameters.name: queryType
parameters.description: Ordering applied to the returned replies: `Relevance` surfaces the replies the platform deems most relevant, `Latest` orders by most recent first, and `Likes` orders by most-liked first.

replies.description: The replies on this page, each a full tweet object.

has_next_page.description: Whether more replies remain beyond the current page.

next_cursor.description: Token to pass back as `cursor` to retrieve the next page of replies.

status.description: Outcome of the request: `success` for a normal result, `error` when the request failed.

message.description: Human-readable error detail, populated only when the request failed.
